variations: 進階版：如果行李遺失，賠償金可以拿來辦健身房年費。
後續：機場海關問我那一圈要申報什麼，我說「免稅的肉」。

en.content:
I figured out a fast way to lose weight: check my fat as checked luggage.
Next time I fly, when the counter says I'm five kilos over, I'll peel the ring off my waist, slap a fragile sticker on it, and send it to the cargo hold.

I'll pick it up on the return flight. This way I don't even need travel insurance.
en.editorNote: Drop into the family chat the moment you book flights, paired with a photo of a lunch you definitely shouldn't have ordered
variations: Upgrade: if the luggage gets lost, the compensation pays for a yearly gym membership.
Follow-up: customs asked what I was declaring. I said 'duty-free meat'.

items.sourceNote: 卡路里追蹤 app 自欺欺人主題重寫，加入台灣便當與手搖飲情境

zh-tw.content:
我下載了卡路里計算 app，今天認真記錄：
早餐 300 大卡、午餐 500 大卡、晚餐 400 大卡。

附註欄寫著：手搖飲、雞排、宵夜、室友烤的餅乾、路過聞到的滷味，這些 app 沒問就不算。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給跟你一起辦健身房又一起放棄的戰友——他會說這就是科學減肥
variations: 進階版：app 通知我今天熱量沒超標，我感動到去買了第二杯珍奶慶祝。
後續：客服回信說 app 沒辦法偵測「視覺攝取」，所以看別人吃不算。

en.content:
I downloaded a calorie tracking app and logged everything honestly today:
Breakfast 300, lunch 500, dinner 400.

In the notes I wrote: bubble tea, fried chicken cutlet, late-night snack, the cookies my roommate baked, the braised stuff I smelled walking past — if the app doesn't ask, it doesn't count.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who signed up for the gym with you and quit with you — they'll call this scientific dieting
variations: Upgrade: the app told me I stayed under my calorie limit today, so I bought a second bubble tea to celebrate.
Follow-up: support emailed back saying the app can't detect 'visual intake', so watching other people eat doesn't count.

items.sourceNote: 「Probably nothing arouses more false hopes than the first four hours of a diet」格式重寫，台灣口語延伸

zh-tw.content:
這個世界上最讓人燃起希望的時刻，是減肥的前四個小時。
那四個小時，你相信自己會在夏天前瘦十公斤、會買到 S 號的牛仔褲、會被前任後悔。

第五個小時開始，你只相信便利商店的鹹酥雞便當。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在早上立完減肥誓言、傍晚打開外送 app 的時候傳給自己
variations: 進階版：第六個小時，你已經在規劃明天要從哪一餐重新開始。
後續：減肥日記第一頁寫滿目標，第二頁開始就只寫菜單。

en.content:
The moment that fills people with the most hope in this world is the first four hours of a diet.
In those four hours, you believe you'll drop ten kilos before summer, fit into a size small, and make your ex regret everything.

Starting in hour five, the only thing you believe in is the fried-chicken bento at the convenience store.
en.editorNote: Send to yourself in the morning after the vow, and again at sunset when the delivery app opens
variations: Upgrade: by hour six, you're already planning which meal tomorrow you'll restart from.
Follow-up: page one of the diet journal is full of goals. From page two it's just menus.

items.sourceNote: 「seafood diet — I see food and I eat it」經典格式，改為台灣手搖飲與夜市情境

zh-tw.content:
我在執行一種叫「目視飲食法」的計畫。
看到食物，我就吃。沒看到，我就找。找不到，我就外送。

營養師說這沒什麼效果，但我覺得它的執行力是我這輩子最高的一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在打開外送 app 的時候傳到減肥群組，配一張你剛剛點完的明細截圖
variations: 進階版：我已經把這套方法寫成電子書，書名叫《看見就贏一半》。
後續：朋友問我能不能教他，我說可以，學費請用炸雞支付。

en.content:
I'm on something called the visual diet.
If I see food, I eat it. If I don't see it, I look for it. If I can't find it, I order delivery.

The nutritionist says it's not effective, but it's the most committed I've ever been to any plan in my life.
en.editorNote: Send to the diet group chat the moment you open the delivery app, paired with a screenshot of your order
variations: Upgrade: I've turned the method into an e-book called 'Seeing Is Half The Battle'.
Follow-up: a friend asked if I'd teach him. I said sure, tuition is payable in fried chicken.

items.sourceNote: 「lost the same ten pounds many times, cellulite has déjà vu」概念重寫，加入台灣體脂機情境

zh-tw.content:
我這輩子減下來的體重加起來大概有 80 公斤，
但目前還是同一個人。

體脂機看到我都會嘆氣，因為它覺得自己被當成電動玩具，每次都是同一關。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在量完體重很沮喪的早上傳給也在減肥的閨蜜，附上一張早餐的拿鐵照
variations: 進階版：我家那台體脂機已經學會自動省略「身體年齡」那一行，怕我看了想退貨。
後續：健身房教練建議我換一台，我說問題不在機器，是我的迴圈程式跑不出去。

en.content:
Across my lifetime I've lost about eighty kilos in total,
but I'm still the same person.

My body composition scale sighs every time it sees me. It feels like a video game cartridge stuck on the same level.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's also dieting on a sad weigh-in morning, ideally with a latte photo attached
variations: Upgrade: my scale at home now auto-hides the 'body age' line because it's worried I'll return it.
Follow-up: my trainer suggested a new model. I told him the bug isn't in the hardware, it's in my loop.

items.sourceNote: 「I'm into fitness, fittin' this pizza in my mouth」雙關概念重寫，改為台灣鹹酥雞情境

zh-tw.content:
別人問我有沒有在運動，我說我很注重「肌動」。
他們以為我在練重訓，其實我說的是「雞動」——
看到鹹酥雞就會自動心跳加速、雙手伸出、口水分泌的那種運動。

燃脂效果普通，但這套動作我已經練到完全反射。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合下班經過夜市的時候傳給戰友，配一句「你要加九層塔嗎」
variations: 進階版：我這套訓練不需要器材，鹹酥雞攤就是我的健身房。
後續：教練問我深蹲幾下，我說看老闆炸幾分鐘。

en.content:
When people ask if I work out, I say I'm really into fryness training.
They think I mean fitness — actually I mean the fried-chicken reflex.
The one where my heart rate spikes, my hands extend, and my salivary glands kick in the second I smell the stall.

Fat-burn results are mediocre, but I have the movement down to pure muscle memory.
en.editorNote: Send to your gym buddy on the walk home past the night market, with a 'extra basil?' tagged on
variations: Upgrade: my training needs zero equipment. The fried-chicken stall is my gym.
Follow-up: my coach asked how many squats I did. I said it depends on how long the oil's been heating.

items.sourceNote: 「gym membership card thinks we're making progress」概念重寫，加入台灣健身房月費情境

zh-tw.content:
我辦了健身房年費，月費自動扣款已經第八個月。
會員卡還在皮夾裡，狀態維持「全新」。

健身房櫃台小姐看到我來都很驚訝，問我「會員是不是要退費」，我說不用，我是來感受月費花掉的那一刻。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在月初被扣款通知吵醒的時候傳到家族群組，配一張你的健身房 APP 截圖
variations: 進階版：我覺得這筆錢不是運動費，是「我有在努力」的心理治療費。
後續：年底續約的時候，櫃台問我要不要改方案，我說維持原樣，這樣比較像我。

en.content:
I bought a yearly gym membership. The monthly auto-debit has gone through eight times.
The card is still in my wallet, condition: brand new.

The front-desk staff looked genuinely shocked when I walked in, and asked if I was here to cancel. I said no, I just came to feel the moment the monthly fee leaves my account.
en.editorNote: Send to the family chat the morning the auto-debit alert wakes you up, ideally with a screenshot of your gym app
variations: Upgrade: I've decided this isn't an exercise fee. It's therapy billed under 'I'm trying'.
Follow-up: at renewal the desk asked if I wanted to switch plans. I said no — keep it the same, it suits me better.

items.sourceNote: 「calories are creatures that sew your clothes tighter at night」概念重寫，加入台灣換季衣櫃情境

zh-tw.content:
我終於知道為什麼牛仔褲越來越緊了——
卡路里其實是一群住在我衣櫃裡的小精靈，
每天晚上趁我睡覺的時候，把我的衣服一件一件偷偷改小。

他們手很巧，連我去年買的那件寬鬆 T 恤都沒放過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合換季整理衣櫃時傳給室友，配一張「這件以前明明很鬆」的試穿照
variations: 進階版：我懷疑他們最近升級了，連褲頭鬆緊帶都不放過。
後續：我打算下次買衣服直接買大兩號，當作預付給精靈的勞務費。

en.content:
I finally figured out why my jeans keep getting tighter.
Calories are actually tiny creatures living in my closet,
and every night while I'm asleep, they go through my wardrobe and take everything in by half an inch.

They're skilled — even the loose T-shirt I bought last year didn't escape.
en.editorNote: Send to your roommate while doing a seasonal closet purge, ideally with a 'this used to be loose' fit check
variations: Upgrade: I think they've leveled up — even the elastic waistbands are getting attention now.
Follow-up: next shopping trip I'm buying two sizes up, basically prepaying the closet creatures for their labor.

items.sourceNote: 「weight loss starts in your head with a decision」概念延伸，加入台灣便利商店掙扎情境

zh-tw.content:
減肥不是從健身房開始的，是從你站在便利商店冰櫃前那一刻開始的。

你伸手拿無糖豆漿，再放回去拿巧克力牛奶，再放回去拿無糖豆漿——
那 30 秒的內心戰爭，就是整個減肥計畫的縮影。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在晚上 10 點便利商店宵夜時間傳給自己看，當作一種儀式
variations: 進階版：今天我贏了，下週可能會輸，但這場仗會一直打下去。
後續：店員看我猶豫太久，默默把巧克力牛奶往前推了一點。

en.content:
Weight loss doesn't start at the gym. It starts the moment you're standing in front of the convenience store fridge.

You reach for the unsweetened soy milk, put it back, grab the chocolate milk, put it back, grab the soy milk again —
those 30 seconds of internal warfare are basically your entire diet plan in miniature.
en.editorNote: Send to yourself at 10pm during the midnight-snack run — treat it like a small ritual
variations: Upgrade: today I won. Next week I might lose. But this fight is ongoing.
Follow-up: the clerk watched me hesitate so long they quietly nudged the chocolate milk forward.

items.sourceNote: 「my favorite exercise is lunch — a cross between a lunge and a crunch」雙關概念，改為台灣下午茶情境

zh-tw.content:
我最常做的運動叫做「午茶深蹲」。
動作分解：彎腰打開蛋糕盒、起身拿叉子、坐下、再起身去拿第二塊。

一組四下，一天三組，三餐後外加宵夜場，年資已滿三年。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合下午三點精神不濟時傳到辦公室群組，配一張誰買的鳳梨酥
variations: 進階版：教練看到我做這套動作，說我核心穩定度其實很好。
後續：我打算下個月把這套動作申請成新的健身派別，名字就叫「茶蹲」。

en.content:
My most consistent workout is called the 'tea-time squat'.
Movement breakdown: bend to open the cake box, stand to grab a fork, sit down, stand back up for the second slice.

Four reps a set, three sets a day, plus a midnight encore. I have three years of seniority in this discipline.
en.editorNote: Send to the office group chat at 3pm energy crash, ideally with 'who brought the pineapple cakes' as a photo
variations: Upgrade: my coach saw the form and said my core stability is actually pretty solid.
Follow-up: I'm filing the paperwork next month to register this as a new fitness discipline. Working title: tea-squat.

items.sourceNote: 「motivation gets you started, habit keeps you going」概念重寫為台灣減肥日常版

zh-tw.content:
動力是你週日晚上立下的志向，習慣是你週三早上六點還是起得來。

動力會在你看到便當的那一秒消失，習慣會幫你自動把白飯換成半碗。

減肥的真相是：動力是煙火，習慣是路燈。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合貼在書桌上、辦公桌前，或健身房置物櫃內側，給未來那個想偷懶的自己看
variations: 進階版：第一週靠動力，第二個月靠習慣，第三年靠的是「不想再從頭來一次」。
後續：今天我沒運動，但我喝了水、睡飽了，習慣的路燈還亮著。

en.content:
Motivation is the goal you set on Sunday night. Habit is the reason you're still up at 6am on Wednesday.

Motivation disappears the second you see takeout. Habit quietly swaps your full rice bowl for a half portion.

The real truth about weight loss: motivation is fireworks, habit is the streetlight.
en.editorNote: Stick it on your desk, your office monitor, or the inside of your gym locker — for the future you who wants to skip a day
variations: Upgrade: week one runs on motivation. Month two runs on habit. Year three runs on 'I refuse to start over again'.
Follow-up: I didn't work out today. But I drank water and slept enough. The habit streetlight is still on.

items.sourceNote: 「I don't do squats. My relationship with gravity is already complicated」原典重寫，加台灣捷運博愛座場景

zh-tw.content:
教練問我為什麼不做深蹲。
我說：我跟地心引力的關係已經夠複雜了，不需要再每天蹲下去確認一次它還愛我。

證據就是——我每次坐下，地球都用比平常多 0.3 秒的時間才把我接住。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次練腿日就裝病的朋友——他會回你「太懂我了」
variations: 後續：今天我坐捷運博愛座，旁邊的阿伯說「年輕人坐得很穩喔」——他不知道我是站不起來。
進階版：我已經把深蹲外包給社區的樓梯間，每天上下五樓，這叫做被動式重訓。

en.content:
My trainer asked why I skip squats.
I told him: my relationship with gravity is already complicated. I don't need to crouch down every day to confirm it still loves me.

The evidence: every time I sit down, the earth takes an extra 0.3 seconds longer than usual to catch me.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who fakes an injury every leg day — they'll reply 'finally, someone gets it'
variations: Follow-up: today an old man on the subway told me 'you sit very steadily for a young person'. He didn't know it's because I can't stand back up.
Pro version: I've outsourced my squats to the apartment stairwell — five floors up and down daily. I call it passive strength training.

items.sourceNote: 「I'd like to thank my personal trainer for the muscles. And my pizza delivery guy for the motivation」原典重寫，加台灣外送員場景

zh-tw.content:
我想感謝兩個人：
感謝教練，給了我肌肉。
感謝外送員，給了我去健身房的動力。

沒有他每週三次準時送到的鹹酥雞，我永遠不會知道自己原來需要運動。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合健身完打卡發限動，配一張運動鞋跟外送收據的併排照
variations: 更新：今天我順便感謝便利商店店員，他賣給我的微波義大利麵，讓我下定決心明天一定要去健身房。
進階版：我已經把外送員列入我的健身夥伴名單——他是我唯一持續超過半年的健身夥伴。

en.content:
I'd like to thank two people:
Thanks to my trainer for the muscles.
Thanks to my delivery driver for the motivation.

Without the fried chicken arriving at my door three times a week, I would never have realized I needed to work out.
en.editorNote: Perfect for a post-workout check-in story — pair with a side-by-side of your trainers and the delivery receipt
variations: Update: today I also want to thank the convenience store clerk. The microwave pasta they sold me convinced me to go to the gym tomorrow.
Pro tier: I've officially added my delivery driver to my fitness buddy list — he's the only fitness buddy I've kept for more than six months.

items.sourceNote: 「I don't need to be perfect to be worthy. I just need to show up」概念重寫為溫柔減肥版，加台灣日常場景

zh-tw.content:
你不需要完美才值得瘦下來。
你今天少點了一杯手搖、樓梯走了多兩層、宵夜換成水煮蛋——

這些不會立刻變成腹肌，但它們會慢慢長成一個更喜歡自己的你。

減肥不是把現在的你丟掉，是讓未來的你多一個選項。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合凌晨低潮時傳給自己，或健身房戰友——這一句不需要圖，直接放著就好
variations: 進階版：減肥的終點不是體重計上的數字，是某天早上你穿衣服時，不再對著鏡子嘆氣。
後續：今天我只多走了一站捷運，但我為這一站感到驕傲——這就夠了。

en.content:
You don't need to be perfect to deserve a smaller version of yourself.
Today you skipped the bubble tea, took the stairs two flights, swapped your midnight ramen for a boiled egg —

these things won't turn into abs by tomorrow, but they'll slowly grow into a version of you who likes yourself a little more.

Dieting isn't about throwing away who you are now. It's about giving future-you one more option.
en.editorNote: Save for a late-night low moment, or send to a gym buddy — this one doesn't need a photo, just let it sit
variations: Pro version: the finish line of dieting isn't the number on the scale. It's a morning when you put on clothes and don't sigh at the mirror.
Follow-up: today I walked one extra subway stop. I'm proud of that one stop. That's enough.

items.sourceNote: 夏天前最後一週減肥衝刺的自嘲，原創語錄；台灣端午粽子在地化

zh-tw.content:
夏天剩兩週，端午節剩四天。
我的減肥計畫遇到了人生中最強對手——肉粽。

它躺在電鍋裡，散發著一種「你已經撐到五月了，沒差這幾顆」的氣息。
我看著它，它看著我，我們達成共識：先過完端午再說。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每年端午都重新立志的朋友——他會邊吃粽子邊回你「我懂」
variations: 更新：吃完三顆肉粽後，我決定把減肥計畫延到中秋——畢竟夏天還很長。
進階版：今年我發明了「節日減肥法」——所有節日都先過完，剩下的日子認真執行，結果一年只剩 47 天。

en.content:
Two weeks until summer. Four days until Dragon Boat Festival.
My diet plan just met its toughest opponent in life — sticky rice dumplings.

They sit in the rice cooker, radiating an energy that says, 'you've made it to May, what's a few more dumplings going to do?'
I look at them, they look at me. We reach an agreement: let's get through the festival first.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who renews their diet vows every holiday — they'll reply mid-dumpling, 'I get it'
variations: Update: after three dumplings, I've officially postponed the diet to Mid-Autumn Festival. Summer's still long.
Pro version: this year I invented the 'holiday-first diet' — finish every holiday first, then get serious. Turns out the year only has 47 actual diet days.

items.sourceNote: 「the only time to eat diet food is while you're waiting for the steak to cook」經典格式重寫，加台灣牛排館場景

zh-tw.content:
減肥餐唯一適合的時機，是你在等牛排上桌的那十分鐘。
那段時間裡，你可以盡情吃沙拉吧、配玉米濃湯、外加兩塊蒜香麵包。

等牛排來了，沙拉只是前菜——這是邏輯，不是放棄。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次去吃牛排都先把沙拉吧掃過三輪的朋友——他會回你「這叫前情提要」
variations: 升級版：沙拉吧再去第四輪的時候，店員看我的眼神已經從尊敬變成了同情。
後續：服務生問我牛排要幾分熟，我說「等我吃完冰淇淋再決定」。

en.content:
The only appropriate time to eat diet food is during the ten minutes you're waiting for your steak.
In that window, you can demolish the salad bar, knock back the corn chowder, and grab two cloves of garlic bread.

When the steak arrives, the salad becomes the appetizer. This is logic, not surrender.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who sweeps the salad bar three times before the entrée arrives — they'll reply 'I call this the prologue'
variations: Upgrade: by the fourth trip to the salad bar, the staff's expression shifts from respect to genuine concern.
Follow-up: the server asked how I wanted the steak cooked. I said 'check back after I finish dessert'.

items.sourceNote: 「該減的不是肥，是慾望」毒雞湯減肥金句反向重寫，加自嘲口吻

zh-tw.content:
別人說：「該減的不是肥，是慾望。」
我認真想了三秒——

如果我能減掉慾望，那我早就不會半夜兩點站在 7-11 的關東煮鍋前面，認真挑高麗菜捲、米血糕、跟兩根熱狗了。

慾望是肥的源頭。但慾望也是我活著的證據。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次看到減肥金句都自動翻譯成宵夜選單的朋友——他會立刻轉發給超商店員
variations: 後續：今晚我嘗試只買一杯無糖綠茶，結果順手加購了兩顆茶葉蛋——這叫做意志力的局部勝利。
毒雞湯升級版：你不是胖，你只是把人生過得很豐盛——這聽起來比較不傷自尊。

en.content:
Other people say, "What you really need to cut isn't fat — it's desire."
I thought about it for three seconds.

If I could cut my desire, I wouldn't be standing in front of the 7-Eleven hot food counter at 2 a.m., carefully choosing between cabbage rolls, pig's blood cake, and two hot dogs.

Desire is the source of fat. But desire is also proof I'm still alive.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who auto-translates every diet quote into a midnight snack menu — they'll forward it straight to the cashier
variations: Follow-up: tonight I tried to only buy unsweetened green tea. I ended up adding two tea eggs at checkout. Call it a partial victory of willpower.
Toxic motivation upgrade: you're not fat, you've just lived life generously. Sounds way easier on the ego.

items.sourceNote: 「the first thing you lose on a diet is your sense of humor」原典重寫，加台灣同事辦公室在地場景

zh-tw.content:
減肥第一個瘦下來的，永遠不是肚子，是脾氣。

第三天我同事跟我借便利貼，我盯著他看了七秒，差點哭出來。
第五天主管說早安，我回他「祝你今天少吃一點」。
第七天我已經被全辦公室列入「最好不要在午餐前找他講話」的名單。

體重沒掉，人緣先掉了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次節食就變得很兇的朋友——他會立刻回你「我同事剛才也閃過」
variations: 升級版：減肥第十天，我已經練成「用眼神拒絕同事的下午茶」的超能力。
後續：今天我終於崩潰，吃了同事帶來的鳳梨酥，全辦公室爆出歡呼——他們以為我終於走出來了。

en.content:
The first thing to shrink on a diet is never your waistline. It's your patience.

Day three, a coworker asked to borrow a sticky note. I stared at him for seven seconds and almost cried.
Day five, my manager said good morning. I replied, "hope you eat less today."
Day seven, the whole office has me on a list labeled 'do not approach before lunch'.

The weight hasn't budged. My reputation already has.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who turns feral every time they start a diet — they'll reply 'a coworker just dodged me'
variations: Day ten upgrade: I've now mastered the power of declining the office afternoon tea cart with a single glare.
Follow-up: I finally cracked today and ate a coworker's pineapple cake. The entire office cheered — they thought I'd escaped a cult.

items.sourceNote: 「I'm allergic to mornings」格式重寫，加台灣晨跑場景的自嘲

zh-tw.content:
我一直想當那種「早上六點起來跑步」的人。
但每次鬧鐘響起來，我都會誠實地問自己一個問題——

「你瘦了會比較開心，還是再睡兩小時比較開心？」

這個問題我已經回答了九年，答案從來沒變過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次都把鬧鐘設好但永遠按掉的朋友——他會回你「我剛才又輸了」
variations: 升級版：今天我終於六點起床了，但只是為了去買早餐店剛開的蛋餅——這也算贏。
後續：我發現晨跑的人都很安靜，因為他們的對手是自己；而我的對手是被窩，比較難贏。

en.content:
I've always wanted to be the kind of person who runs at 6 a.m.
But every time the alarm goes off, I ask myself one honest question —

"will you be happier thinner, or happier with two more hours of sleep?"

I've been answering this question for nine years. The answer has never changed.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who sets six alarms and snoozes all of them — they'll reply 'I lost again just now'
variations: Upgrade: today I actually got up at 6, but only to grab the first breakfast crepe of the day. Still counts as a win.
Follow-up: morning runners are quiet because their opponent is themselves. My opponent is the duvet — much harder to beat.

items.sourceNote: 夏天前最後衝刺與夏季短褲焦慮格式，原創語錄；台灣熱浪在地化

zh-tw.content:
天氣預報說：明天三十四度，未來一週都是夏天了。
我打開衣櫃，去年的短褲認真地看著我。

我說：「我們今年再相處看看。」
短褲說：「沒事，你站著穿可以，坐下就不行。」

人類跟衣服的關係，比任何感情都要誠實。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合五月底打開衣櫃翻夏裝那一刻發限動，配一張穿不下短褲的腿部特寫
variations: 更新：今天我成功扣上短褲扣子，但坐下的瞬間，扣子彈出去打到電視——它選擇了自由。
後續：我決定今年夏天就走「飄逸長裙風」，這叫做穿搭策略，不叫逃避。

en.content:
The weather app says: 34°C tomorrow, summer for the rest of the week.
I open the closet. Last year's shorts stare at me.

I say, "let's try this one more year."
The shorts say, "standing up is fine. Sitting down is not."

The relationship between humans and clothes is more honest than any relationship I've ever had.
en.editorNote: Perfect for the late-May moment you finally open the summer drawer, paired with a close-up of shorts that refuse to button
variations: Update: today I got the button closed. The second I sat down, the button shot off and hit the TV. It chose freedom.
Follow-up: I've decided this summer is a 'flowy long skirts' summer. This is called a styling strategy, not an escape plan.

items.sourceNote: 間歇性斷食「進食窗口」變得失控的格式重寫，加台灣便利商店與宵夜在地化

zh-tw.content:
我開始做間歇性斷食，理論上只能在一個八小時的「進食窗口」裡吃東西。
問題是，我的窗口從中午開到凌晨，中間還跨了一趟便利商店、兩攤鹹酥雞。

醫生問我窗口幾點關，我說：「等我睡著它就會自己關了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給嘴上說在斷食、實際從早吃到晚的朋友——他會回你「我的窗口是落地窗」
variations: 進階版：我的進食窗口最近升級成「進食落地窗」，再不控制就要變成「進食整面玻璃帷幕」了。
後續：我問營養師窗口可不可以分期付款，她沉默了很久。

en.content:
I started intermittent fasting. In theory I can only eat inside one eight-hour 'eating window'.
The problem is, my window opens at noon and doesn't close until 2 a.m., with a convenience store run and two fried-chicken stalls in between.

The doctor asked when my window closes. I said, 'it closes by itself once I fall asleep.'
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who claims to be fasting but eats from morning to midnight — they'll reply 'my window is a sliding glass door'
variations: Pro version: my eating window has been upgraded to an eating patio door. If I don't get it under control it's becoming a full glass curtain wall.
Follow-up: I asked my nutritionist if the window could be paid off in installments. She went quiet for a long time.

items.sourceNote: 「protein shake tastes like optimism blended with chalk」格式重寫，加台灣手搖飲對照在地化

zh-tw.content:
我認真買了一大罐乳清蛋白，說要替代手搖飲。
第一口下去，我終於懂了——這個味道，是把「上進心」跟「石膏粉」一起打成漿。

喝完第三天，我獎勵自己一杯珍奶半糖去冰，喝的當下我哭了，不是因為糖，是因為終於有味道。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次說要戒手搖、結果買了高蛋白還是偷喝的朋友——他會立刻認同這個味道
variations: 進階版：我把高蛋白加進珍奶裡，想說兩全其美，結果它同時失去了健康跟好喝。
後續：那罐高蛋白現在被我拿來當啞鈴，至少這樣它有在幫我減肥。

en.content:
I committed to a giant tub of whey protein to replace my bubble tea habit.
First sip and I finally understood — this flavor is what happens when you blend ambition with powdered chalk.

On day three I rewarded myself with a half-sugar bubble tea, and I cried mid-sip. Not because of the sugar. Because it finally had a taste.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps quitting bubble tea, buys protein powder, and sneaks the tea anyway — they'll vouch for the flavor
variations: Pro version: I added the protein powder to my bubble tea for the best of both worlds. It instantly lost both the health and the taste.
Follow-up: that protein tub is now my dumbbell. At least this way it's actually helping me lose weight.

items.sourceNote: 「I ordered salad, then remembered who I am and upgraded to fries」格式重寫，加台灣自助餐／便當在地化

zh-tw.content:
我走進自助餐，立志今天只夾燙青菜跟一塊雞胸。
夾到第三樣的時候，我突然想起了我是誰——

於是我的餐盤上多了滷排骨、半熟蛋、爌肉，還有一勺淋了滿滿肉燥的白飯。

青菜還在，它孤單地躺在角落，像個提醒。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次進自助餐都「只夾青菜」結果端出一座肉山的朋友——他會回你「這是我今天的午餐」
variations: 進階版：阿姨幫我打菜的時候多舀了一勺肉燥，我沒有阻止——這不是我的錯，這是台灣人的禮貌。
後續：結帳的時候老闆看了一眼我的盤子，默默把「健康餐」的牌子收起來了。

en.content:
I walked into the cafeteria determined to grab only steamed greens and one piece of chicken breast.
By the third item, I remembered who I am —

and my tray suddenly held braised pork ribs, a soft-boiled egg, stewed pork belly, and a scoop of rice drowning in meat sauce.

The greens are still there, lying alone in the corner like a warning.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who enters the buffet 'for just vegetables' and leaves with a mountain of meat — they'll reply 'that's literally my lunch today'
variations: Pro version: the server added an extra scoop of meat sauce and I didn't stop her. This isn't my fault. This is Taiwanese politeness.
Follow-up: at checkout the owner glanced at my tray and quietly took down the 'healthy meal' sign.

items.sourceNote: 「My cheat day is every day that ends with y」格式重寫，加台灣作弊日／放縱日在地化

zh-tw.content:
我的減肥計畫有一個彈性很大的「作弊日」。
原本設定一週一天，後來變成週末兩天，再後來凡是名字裡有「一二三四五六日」的日子，都可以作弊。

我攤開行事曆認真研究，發現一週七天剛好全部符合條件。
所以嚴格來說，我每天都很守規矩。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給「作弊日比減肥日還多」的朋友——他會回你「那我們根本天天合格」
variations: 進階版：我後來把作弊日改成「獎勵日」，聽起來比較正向，效果一模一樣。
後續：朋友問我哪天是減肥日，我認真想了三分鐘，回答：「應該是去年某一天。」

en.content:
My diet plan has a very flexible 'cheat day'.
It started as one day a week, then became both weekend days, then any day with a name ending in 'y'.

I laid out the calendar and studied it carefully. All seven days of the week qualify.
So technically, I follow the rules every single day.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose cheat days outnumber their diet days — they'll reply 'so we both pass every day'
variations: Pro version: I later renamed it 'reward day' to sound more positive. Same effect, better branding.
Follow-up: a friend asked which day was my diet day. I thought hard for three minutes and answered, 'probably some day last year.'

items.sourceNote: 「Me: I want abs. Also me: I want pizza」雙重內心戲格式重寫，加台灣鹹酥雞／滷味在地化

zh-tw.content:
我：我想要馬甲線。
也是我：我想要鹹酥雞加雞排加滷味大全配。

我：我想要在夏天穿得好看。
也是我：我想要在沙發上躺成一隻吐司。

這兩個我每天都在開會，但從來沒有人退讓，最後通常是鹹酥雞那邊獲勝。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天都在跟自己內心拔河的朋友——他會回你「今天又是鹹酥雞贏了」
variations: 更新：今天兩個我終於和解了，協議內容是「先吃鹹酥雞，馬甲線明年再說」。
後續：我發現想要馬甲線的那個我，通常只在剛洗完澡照鏡子的那三分鐘出現。

en.content:
Me: I want abs.
Also me: I want fried chicken, a chicken cutlet, and the full braised-snack platter.

Me: I want to look good this summer.
Also me: I want to lie on the couch flattened like a slice of toast.

These two versions of me meet every day. Neither ever backs down. The fried chicken side usually wins.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend in a daily tug-of-war with themselves — they'll reply 'fried chicken won again today'
variations: Update: the two of me finally reached a settlement today. The terms: eat the fried chicken now, revisit the abs next year.
Follow-up: I've noticed the version of me that wants abs only shows up for the three minutes I'm looking in the mirror after a shower.

items.sourceNote: 「I stepped on the scale and it asked for my username and password」格式重寫，加台灣體重計在地化

zh-tw.content:
今天早上我踏上體重計。
它沉默了一下，螢幕跳出一行字：「請先登入帳號密碼，並升級為旗艦會員。」

我退後一步，它又補了一句：「另建議綁定信用卡，方便後續分期。」

從那天起，我跟體重計變成了一種，很有距離感的關係。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天早上量體重就心情低落的朋友——他會回你「我已經一個月沒登入了」
variations: 後續：我換了一台新的體重計，這台比較貼心，量完直接顯示「資料維護中，請稍後再試」——它選擇保護我的心情。
進階版：我決定改用「目測法」量體重，誤差很大，但心情非常穩定。

en.content:
I stepped on the scale this morning.
It went quiet for a second, then displayed a line of text: 'Please log in. Premium membership required for full access.'

I stepped back. It added: 'We also recommend linking a credit card for future installment plans.'

From that day on, the scale and I have kept a respectful distance.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose mood crashes after the morning weigh-in — they'll reply 'I haven't logged in for a month'
variations: Follow-up: I bought a new scale. This one is more considerate — it just shows 'data under maintenance, try again later'. It chose to protect my feelings.
Pro version: I've switched to estimating my weight by eye. The margin of error is huge, but my mood is very stable.

items.sourceNote: 「My warm-up is longer than my workout, I'm just here for the snacks」格式重寫，加台灣健身房在地化

zh-tw.content:
我的熱身時間，比我正式運動的時間還長。
滑手機十分鐘、調整一下髮帶、拍兩張限動、研究器材說明書、跟櫃檯閒聊——

等我真正準備好的時候，剛好到了該去買蛋白飲的時間。

所以嚴格來說，我每次都有來健身房，只是沒怎麼健身。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次去健身房都拍照拍最久的朋友——他會回你「重點是儀式感」
variations: 進階版：我把『拍限動』算進有氧訓練，畢竟舉手機那個動作，做久了肩膀也會痠。
後續：教練問我今天練了什麼，我認真回答『主要練了等待』——等器材、等心情、等下班。

en.content:
My warm-up takes longer than my actual workout.
Ten minutes scrolling my phone, adjusting my headband, posting two stories, reading the equipment manual, chatting with the front desk —

by the time I'm truly ready, it's conveniently time to go buy a protein shake.

So technically, I show up to the gym every time. I just don't do much gym.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who spends the most time taking photos at the gym — they'll reply 'it's about the ritual'
variations: Pro version: I count 'posting stories' as cardio. Holding the phone up that long, your shoulders genuinely start to ache.
Follow-up: my trainer asked what I worked on today. I honestly answered, 'mostly waiting' — for the machine, for the mood, for closing time.

items.sourceNote: 「I don't skip leg day, I skip every day that involves legs」格式重寫，加台灣健身房在地化

zh-tw.content:
有人問我會不會偷懶不練腿日。
我說怎麼會，我超有原則的——

我不是只跳過腿日，我是公平地跳過每一個需要用到腿的日子。

胸日、背日、肩日，只要它名字裡有『日』，我一律一視同仁地請假。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次都說『今天剛好休息日』的健身房朋友——他會回你『我們是同一種人』
variations: 進階版：我最認真執行的訓練是『臥推』——平躺在沙發上，把遙控器推來推去。
後續：教練幫我排了一週六練，我看完課表，默默把它收進『勵志收藏』資料夾，再也沒打開。

en.content:
Someone asked if I skip leg day.
I said, never — I have strong principles.

I don't just skip leg day. I fairly skip every single day that involves legs.

Chest day, back day, shoulder day — as long as its name ends in 'day', I take a uniform, impartial absence.
en.editorNote: Send to the gym friend who always says 'today's conveniently a rest day' — they'll reply 'we're the same species'
variations: Pro version: the exercise I'm most committed to is the bench press — lying flat on the couch, pressing the remote back and forth.
Follow-up: my trainer built me a six-day-a-week plan. I read it once, quietly filed it under 'inspirational saves', and never opened it again.

items.sourceNote: 「I think about food 90% of the time」格式重寫，加台灣美食內心戲在地化

zh-tw.content:
別人看我安靜地坐在那邊，以為我在思考人生大事。

其實我腦中正在進行一場激烈的辯論：
晚餐到底要吃麻辣鍋、鹹酥雞，還是乾脆兩個都來。

我清醒的時間裡，大概有九成都在想等一下要吃什麼——
剩下那一成，是在想剛剛那餐有沒有吃夠。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給開會放空時都在想晚餐的朋友——他會回你『被你說中了』
variations: 進階版：我的大腦有一個專門負責規劃三餐的部門，運轉效率比我工作時高出十倍。
後續：朋友問我在想什麼，我說『沒事』，其實我正在心裡比較兩家便當店的滷蛋大小。

en.content:
People see me sitting there quietly and assume I'm pondering life's big questions.

In reality, an intense debate is raging in my head:
should dinner be hot pot, fried chicken, or honestly, just both.

Roughly ninety percent of my waking hours go to thinking about what to eat next —
the remaining ten percent is spent wondering if the last meal was enough.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who zones out in meetings planning dinner — they'll reply 'you called me out'
variations: Pro version: my brain has a dedicated department for meal planning. It runs ten times more efficiently than I do at work.
Follow-up: a friend asked what I was thinking about. I said 'nothing'. I was actually comparing the braised-egg sizes at two different lunch box shops.

items.sourceNote: 「Burpees? I thought you said slurpees」諧音梗格式重寫，加台灣手搖飲在地化

zh-tw.content:
教練在課表上寫了『波比跳三組』。
我看成『波霸奶三杯』，立刻覺得這堂課好有誠意。

等我興高采烈地問他要不要去糖，他沉默了五秒，
說：『同學，那是運動，不是點餐。』

從此我對健身的熱情，下降了大概半糖的程度。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次看到運動名稱都先聯想到食物的朋友——他會回你『所以你也是』
variations: 進階版：後來我發現『深蹲』唸快一點很像『甜甜』，於是我每蹲一下就更想吃甜點，這堂課完全失控。
後續：教練最後妥協了，他說只要我認真做完，下課可以一起去買手搖——這是我運動史上最強的動力來源。

en.content:
My trainer wrote 'three sets of burpees' on the workout plan.
I read it as 'three cups of boba', and suddenly the class felt full of heart.

When I cheerfully asked him whether I should get them less-sweet, he went silent for five seconds and said,
'That's an exercise, not a drink order.'

My enthusiasm for fitness dropped by roughly half-sugar that day.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who hears any exercise name and immediately thinks of food — they'll reply 'so you're one of us too'
variations: Pro version: I later realized 'squats' sounds a bit like 'snacks' if you say it fast, so every rep made me crave dessert more. The class went fully off the rails.
Follow-up: my trainer finally caved and said if I finished the set seriously, we could grab bubble tea after. Strongest source of motivation in my entire fitness history.

items.sourceNote: 「one salad and one workout later, expecting miracles」夏日身材落差格式重寫，加台灣夏天場景

zh-tw.content:
我今天吃了一份沙拉，又做了二十下深蹲。
做完立刻轉身照鏡子，期待看到全新的自己。

鏡子裡的人跟早上一模一樣，只是現在比較喘。

夏天剩不到一個月，但我的身材顯然還活在去年的冬天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次運動完就照鏡子等奇蹟的朋友——他會回你『所以為什麼還沒瘦』
variations: 進階版：我把那份沙拉的照片存起來，準備夏天結束後拿出來說『我有努力過』。
後續：第二天我又吃了一份沙拉，這次連深蹲都省了，反正鏡子也不會給面子。

en.content:
Today I ate one salad and did twenty squats.
Then I spun around to the mirror, expecting to meet a brand-new me.

The person in the mirror looked exactly like this morning, just slightly more out of breath.

Less than a month until summer, and my body is clearly still living in last winter.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who checks the mirror for miracles right after a workout — they'll reply 'so why am I not thin yet'
variations: Pro version: I saved a photo of that salad to pull out after summer and say 'I did try.'
Follow-up: day two I ate another salad and skipped the squats entirely — the mirror wasn't going to cut me any slack anyway.

items.sourceNote: 「the scale asked for my username and password」體重計擬人化格式重寫，加台灣場景

zh-tw.content:
我早上站上體重計，它沉默了好久，
然後緩緩跳出一行字：「請輸入帳號密碼。」

我不知道哪來的資料量，需要登入才看得完。

後來我們達成共識：以後我不站，它也不顯示，彼此留點尊嚴。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天早上跟體重計鬥智的朋友——他會回你『我們也分手了』
variations: 進階版：第二天它直接顯示『系統維護中』，我知道它只是不想面對現實。
後續：我把體重計收進櫃子，它在裡面震了一下——那是如釋重負。

en.content:
I stepped on the scale this morning. It went silent for a long time,
then slowly displayed: 'please enter your username and password.'

I don't know what data volume requires a login to view in full.

We later reached an agreement: I don't step on, it doesn't display, and we both keep our dignity.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend locked in a daily standoff with their scale — they'll reply 'we broke up too'
variations: Pro version: day two it just showed 'system under maintenance'. I knew it simply didn't want to face reality.
Follow-up: I put the scale away in the cabinet. It buzzed once inside — that was relief.

items.sourceNote: 「I do squats so my snacks have a nice shelf」健身房自嘲格式重寫，加台灣零食場景

zh-tw.content:
有人問我為什麼這麼認真練深蹲。
我說：因為這樣我的肚子才有一個穩固的層架，
可以放鹹酥雞、放手搖飲、放整袋洋芋片。

練的不是身材，是收納——這叫做機能性訓練。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每次運動完都要配零食慶祝的朋友——他會說『原來我一直在練收納』
variations: 進階版：今天我加練核心，是為了讓那個層架更穩，珍奶放上去才不會晃。
後續：朋友看完說想跟我一起練，我說好，下課我們先去買零食量一下尺寸。

en.content:
Someone asked why I take squats so seriously.
I said: so my stomach has a sturdy shelf
to hold fried chicken, bubble tea, and a whole bag of chips.

I'm not training my body. I'm training storage. Call it functional fitness.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who celebrates every workout with snacks — they'll say 'turns out I've been training storage all along'
variations: Pro version: today I added core work to make the shelf more stable, so the bubble tea doesn't wobble when I set it down.
Follow-up: a friend said they want to train with me. I said sure — after class we'll buy snacks first to measure the dimensions.

items.sourceNote: 「I'm not addicted to sugar, we're in a committed relationship」食物擬人戀愛格式重寫，加台灣手搖飲場景

zh-tw.content:
別人說我對糖上癮，我覺得這個說法太不尊重了。
我跟糖不是上癮的關係，我們是穩定交往的伴侶。

每天下午三點，手搖飲準時報到，從不放我鴿子。
你說，這種對象去哪裡找？
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天下午都要一杯手搖才能活的朋友——他會回你『這就是真愛』
variations: 進階版：我們最近還決定同居了，現在冰箱裡長期備著珍奶跟布丁——這叫做交往的承諾。
後續：朋友勸我分手，我說可以啊，但要等他先幫我找到一個一樣準時又一樣甜的對象。

en.content:
People say I'm addicted to sugar. I find that deeply disrespectful.
Sugar and I aren't an addiction. We're a committed couple in a stable relationship.

Every day at three p.m., bubble tea shows up on time and never stands me up.
Tell me, where else would I find a partner like that?
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who can't function without an afternoon bubble tea — they'll reply 'this is true love'
variations: Pro version: we recently decided to move in together. The fridge is now permanently stocked with bubble tea and pudding — that's commitment.
Follow-up: a friend told me to break up. I said sure, right after they find me someone equally punctual and equally sweet.
